rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Follow AADL scope rules for AGREE annex placement
Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope
Avoid inventing declarations for unresolved references
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
Use `guarantee "label": expression;` for output dependency contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not invent declarations for unresolved references
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 2
